opp.next_step: 16th of June - Confidence level : 25% Close date postponed to mid of February 27. Email from Antoine (SPOC and IT purchaser) response to my mail from 15th of June - No Action before September 26 (Useless) The decision of launching an RFP has been postponed to Q3 2026 (maybe even Q4) considering the current maturity of our ERP project. Once decisions are taken on this matter, we'll be able to determine a potential scope and launch an RFP on the S2P. 15th of June - Confidence level : 25% To chase them and obtain confirmation about RFP planning. Close date updated accordingly. Reason : ERP project is a priority. I mentioned that we can start with Downstream / Low or No impact on ERP in a First Phase and deploy P2P after. To Chase again in June (not before useless) 06 - January - 2025 - Confidence level : 25% Clear NS / Take the opportunity of NY to call and obtain confirmation of timing. opp.ais_why:
• Avoma demo on 5 Dec 2025 with Mantu attendees Jeremy COURDIL, SELMANI Assia and Antoine Rodigue explicitly covered AI-driven intake routing via natural language, an Agentic AI layer with 45+ agents for autonomous supplier discovery / risk / onboarding / tail-spend, and Merlin Intake embedded in Teams for zero-training. Zycus committed in the call to send an RFI response including AI agent details ‚Äî the RFI explicitly requires AI capability detail, a strong evaluation-criterion signal.
• Formal Evaluation stage at $350K with broad AI-rich product scope (ANA, Agentic AI, Merlin, Merlin Intake) plus the full S2P suite (eInvoicing, eProcurement, iContract, iRequest, iRisk, iSave, iSupplier). 2-hour demo with Pierre Meraud, Brice Phosarajdavong and Hemendra Chaturvedi presenting. RFI underway with technical workshop scheduled. Customer Antoine engaged with detailed questions on agentic flow. Vienna March 10-12 customer event invitation extended.
• High confidence: direct AI-feature demo with named customer-side engagement, an RFI explicitly requiring AI capability detail, and AI-focused product scope. Held at 8 because BD_Engagement is Unengaged in SFDC (data lag ‚Äî engagement is clearly real) and no signed POC scope yet. A formal POC commitment or RFI shortlist confirmation would push to 9.

sources: Avoma product demo 5 December 2025, speaker_id 3: tail-spend management 2-3% savings
Avoma 5 December 2025 key takeaway: unified S2P suite 2-3% additional savings aim
metrics.narrative: Mantu articulated tail-spend management as a key value driver: capturing the non-strategic 20% of spend to achieve 2-3% additional savings (source: Avoma product demo 5 December 2025, speaker_id 3). The unified Source-to-Pay suite aim is also 2-3% additional savings on unmanaged tail spend (source: Avoma 5 December 2025, key takeaway). However, no buyer-stated quantified baseline spend, current cost leakage, headcount savings, or cycle-time improvement has been captured. The business problem remains at the qualitative level (tail-spend leakage, ERP integration complexity). The buyer has not yet articulated their own ROI case or quantified the pain in their own terms. Because the RFP has not launched, detailed scoping and quantified business case development have not occurred. The next step to close this gap is to co-develop a quantified value model with Antoine Rodigue and the procurement lead once the ERP project roadmap clarifies and RFP scoping begins in September 2026.

sources: Avoma 5 December 2025: Nicolas EUCHER and Jérémy COURDIL engaged in product demo, asked functional questions, no advocacy evidence
Next_Step__c 16 June 2026: Antoine Rodigue named as SPOC, sent deferral email with no advocacy or internal selling
No evidence of champion introductions, competitive intelligence sharing, or internal selling across Avoma or Salesforce
champion.narrative: No strong champion has been identified. The buyer participants on the 5 December 2025 product demo (Nicolas EUCHER, Procurement Manager, and Jérémy COURDIL, Data Quality Manager) asked functional questions and were engaged during the demo, but no evidence of advocacy, internal selling, or strategic access has been captured (source: Avoma 5 December 2025). Antoine Rodigue (IT Category Manager) is named as the SPOC and IT purchaser in Next_Step__c, but he has not appeared on any recorded call, and his 16 June 2026 email was a gatekeeper message deferring all action to September 2026 (source: Next_Step__c 16 June 2026). No buyer contact has introduced Zycus to the Economic Buyer, shared internal competitive intelligence, driven the RFP timeline, or pushed back on the ERP-driven delay. The buyer is engaged but passive; Zycus is the pursuer. This is a developing champion situation at best, more accurately a functional point-of-contact relationship with no confirmed advocacy. The risk is high that once the RFP launches in Q3/Q4 2026, multiple vendors will compete on equal footing with no internal advocate tilting the evaluation toward Zycus. The top priority once buyer re-engagement begins in September 2026 is to identify and cultivate a true champion (ideally at Head of Procurement or CPO level) who has power, access to the Economic Buyer, and a vested interest in Zycus winning.

sources: Salesforce Competitors__c: empty
Next_Step__c history: no competitor mentioned across all entries
Avoma 5 December 2025: no competitor discussed
Next_Step__c 28 November 2025: formal global RFP on S2P received, implying multi-vendor evaluation, but no names disclosed
competition.narrative: No competitor has been named in Salesforce (Competitors__c is empty), in any Next_Step__c entry, in any Salesforce Task, or in the single Avoma call transcript from 5 December 2025. The buyer issued a formal RFI in November 2025, which implies a multi-vendor evaluation, but the competitive shortlist has not been disclosed or discovered. This is a critical blind spot. In a formal RFP cycle for a €350k S2P suite in Europe, likely competitors include SAP Ariba, Coupa, Ivalua, GEP, Jaggaer, and Basware, but none have been confirmed. The absence of competitive intelligence this deep into the cycle (7 months old, post-RFI) is a significant risk. Once the buyer re-engages in September 2026 and the RFP is issued, Zycus must explicitly ask Antoine Rodigue or Nicolas EUCHER who else is being evaluated, map their strengths/weaknesses, and develop the competitive wedge (Merlin agentic AI, unified single-database architecture, Microsoft Teams embedding) to differentiate. The recommended move is to run a competitive discovery conversation as soon as the RFP timeline resumes.

sources: Avoma 5 December 2025: tail-spend management to capture non-strategic 20% spend, 2-3% savings
Avoma 5 December 2025: autonomous supplier onboarding, AI intake via Teams for zero-training adoption, unified S2P on single database
Avoma 5 December 2025: buyer questions on ERP integration, compliance automation, invoice processing
identify_pain.narrative: Mantu's pain is tail-spend leakage and lack of unified procurement process management across their portfolio. The buyer explicitly explored tail-spend management to capture the non-strategic 20% of spend and achieve 2-3% additional savings (source: Avoma 5 December 2025, speaker_id 3 use case). The buyer's interest in autonomous supplier onboarding, AI-driven intake via the relevant stakeholder for zero-training adoption, and unified Source-to-Pay on a single database all point to current-state fragmentation: manual supplier onboarding, disconnected tools, and low user adoption driving maverick spend (source: Avoma 5 December 2025 key takeaways). The buyer asked detailed questions about ERP integration, compliance automation (sanctions/embargo screening, contract clause validation), and invoice processing mailbox integration, signaling pain around ERP complexity, compliance risk exposure, and AP inefficiency (source: Avoma 5 December 2025). However, no named pain owner (the executive sponsor of the S2P transformation) has been identified. The operational pain is clear and articulated by the mid-level team; the strategic/financial pain owner and quantified business case remain to be developed once the RFP scoping resumes in Q3 2026.

sources: Avoma 5 December 2025: buyer questions on eSignature integrations and compliance certifications
No Salesforce field or Avoma content on MSA/SOW, legal review, InfoSec, or SI involvement
paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics (MSA, SOW, order form), legal review cycles, InfoSec questionnaire, procurement channel (direct vs via partner/SI), and implementation scoping formalization are all unknown. No discussion of paper process, legal redlines, security review, or SI involvement has been captured in Avoma or Salesforce. The buyer asked about eSignature integrations (Zycus Certinal, Adobe Sign, DocuSign) and compliance certifications (GDPR, security certs) on the 5 December 2025 call, signaling awareness of contracting and compliance requirements, but no formal paper-process walkthrough has occurred (source: Avoma 5 December 2025). Because the RFP has not launched and the deal is pre-shortlist, paper process definition is premature. Once shortlisted and moving to vendor selection, Zycus must map Mantu's procurement policy, legal/InfoSec gate timeline, MSA/SOW template requirements, and any SI/implementation partner involvement to avoid late-stage delays.

sources: Avoma 5 December 2025 attendees: Nicolas EUCHER (Procurement Manager), Jérémy COURDIL (Data Quality Manager)
Next_Step__c 16 June 2026: Antoine Rodigue named as SPOC and IT purchaser
Salesforce OpportunityContactRole: no Economic Buyer or Executive Sponsor role assigned
economic_buyer.narrative: No Economic Buyer has been identified or engaged. No Salesforce Contact Role is marked Economic Buyer or Executive Sponsor. The only buyer participants on the 5 December 2025 product demo were Nicolas EUCHER (Procurement Manager) and Jérémy COURDIL (Data Quality Manager), both mid-level functional roles (source: Avoma 5 December 2025 attendees inferred from speaker interactions). Antoine Rodigue (IT Category Manager) is the stated SPOC per Next_Step__c but has not been on a recorded call. No CFO, CPO, or C-level procurement/finance sponsor has been named or engaged. Budget authority, final commercial sign-off, and contract approval pathways are unknown. The Account-level contact list includes a Head of Procurement role (not yet confirmed as involved in this S2P opportunity), but no executive-level engagement has occurred. This is a critical gap for a €350k deal. The recommended move is to map the Economic Buyer and secure an executive sponsor introduction once the RFP scope is defined and the buyer re-engages in September 2026.

sources: Next_Step__c 8 December 2025, 6 January 2026: RFP June 2026, decision contract Q4 2026
Next_Step__c 16 June 2026: RFP postponed to Q3 2026 (possibly Q4), dependent on ERP project decisions
Avoma 5 December 2025 attendees: only Nicolas EUCHER and Jérémy COURDIL on buyer side, no executive or decision-maker present
decision_process.narrative: The buyer's process is RFI (completed December 2025) → RFP (planned Q3 or Q4 2026) → shortlisting → vendor selection → contract decision Q4 2026 (source: Next_Step__c 8 December 2025, 6 January 2026). The RFP launch has been postponed from June 2026 to September 2026 or later, contingent on the maturity of Mantu's ERP project; once ERP decisions are made, the S2P scope and RFP will be finalized (source: Next_Step__c 16 June 2026, email from Antoine Rodigue). The decision timeline and approval chain beyond the stated contract decision Q4 2026 are unclear. No multi-stage approval process, no named decision committee members, and no Commercial / Legal / InfoSec gate sequence has been documented. The single 5 December 2025 call had only two mid-level buyer participants (Nicolas EUCHER, Jérémy COURDIL); no executive, no procurement head, no IT leadership, no finance/legal appeared. This signals that formal process definition and stakeholder assembly are still early. The process is real (formal RFI/RFP cycle) but not yet fully visible. The next step is to map the decision committee composition and approval gates once the RFP is issued in Q3/Q4 2026.

sources: Next_Step__c 28 November 2025: formal global RFP on S2P received, RFI answer due 12 December
Next_Step__c 8 December 2025: RFI answer to be sent by 12 December
Avoma 5 December 2025: buyer questions on supplier onboarding, ERP integration, compliance, AI intake, contract validation, Teams-based invoice processing
decision_criteria.narrative: A formal RFI was issued by Mantu in late November 2025 and Zycus submitted the RFI response by 12 December 2025 (source: Next_Step__c 28 November 2025, 8 December 2025). The RFI content and buyer's evaluation criteria have not been captured in Salesforce or Avoma transcripts. On the 5 December 2025 product demo, Mantu asked detailed questions about supplier onboarding automation, tail-spend dashboards, ERP integration, AI intake routing, compliance (GDPR, security certifications), sanctions/embargo screening, contract clause validation, and invoice processing via Teams (source: Avoma 5 December 2025 questions and use-cases). These topics signal the buyer's functional priorities: autonomous supplier onboarding, risk/compliance rigor, Microsoft Teams integration, and ERP bi-directional sync. However, the formal weighted decision criteria, scoring model, and must-have vs nice-to-have requirements have not been documented. The buyer has postponed RFP launch to Q3/Q4 2026, so formal criteria definition is pending. Once the RFP is issued, Zycus must capture the evaluation scorecard, decision committee composition, and technical vs commercial weighting to align the response and demo strategy.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AI appetite confirmed at functional level. Buyer asked detailed questions about AI-guided intake routing, autonomous supplier onboarding, AI contract review for compliance, and Merlin agents during the 5 December 2025 demo, and provided 'super positive feedback.' The full S2P product scope includes Merlin, Merlin Intake, and Agentic AI per Products field. However, no AIS (AI Score/Status/Why) has been populated in Salesforce, so executive-level AI appetite and strategic AI priority are unconfirmed. The mid-level team is AI-curious and engaged with the agentic AI narrative; strategic AI hunger awaits Economic Buyer and C-level engagement once RFP resumes in Q3/Q4 2026.

deal_movement.summary: Deal created 25 November 2025 upon receipt of formal RFI. Zycus delivered comprehensive product demo 5 December 2025 and submitted RFI response by 12 December 2025. Buyer planned RFP launch June 2026, contract decision Q4 2026. RFP timeline slipped to Q3 2026 (September) in February 2026 due to ERP project taking priority. Further confirmed 16 June 2026 that RFP launch postponed to Q3 or possibly Q4 2026, dependent on ERP project maturity, with explicit instruction from Antoine Rodigue: no action before September 2026. Deal is live (recent contact 10 days ago) but stalled on a buyer-side dependency. No stage progression beyond Formal Evaluation in 7 months.

items.detail: No Economic Buyer, no Executive Sponsor, no C-level procurement or finance engagement in 7 months. All buyer interactions have been with mid-level functional roles (Procurement Manager, Data Quality Manager, IT Category Manager). Budget authority, contract approval pathway, and strategic sponsorship are unknown. For a €350k S2P transformation, executive alignment is critical and currently absent.

Single-threaded into mid-level functional contacts with no confirmed champion or advocate. Only 2 buyer-side OpportunityContactRoles; no multi-threading to decision committee, Economic Buyer, or C-level. High risk that other vendors will gain stronger executive sponsorship and champion advocacy once RFP launches.

No competitive intelligence. Buyer issued formal RFI November 2025 signaling multi-vendor evaluation, but competitive shortlist unknown 7 months into cycle. Blind to competitor strengths, weaknesses, and buyer sentiment. Cannot tailor positioning or wedge strategy without competitive visibility.

confidence_signals.summary: Deal is live (buyer contact 10 days ago) but momentum is paused on a buyer-side dependency (ERP project decisions). Buyer confidence level has remained at 25% since January 2026 across all Next_Step entries, signaling low probability and high uncertainty. Positive signal: buyer provided 'super positive feedback' on 5 December 2025 demo and completed formal RFI process. Negative signals: RFP postponed twice (June 2026 to Q3 2026 to possibly Q4 2026); no stage progression in 7 months; no Economic Buyer or executive engagement; single-threaded into passive functional contacts; no competitive intelligence; no champion. The deal is not cooling or going dark (recent contact, buyer explicitly stated timing), but it is stalled and vulnerable once the RFP launches due to lack of executive alignment, champion, and competitive visibility.

north_star_verdict.math: 234 days to close date (2027-02-15). Deal created 2025-11-25, now ~7 months old. In Formal Evaluation since creation. Buyer explicitly stated on 16 June 2026 that RFP launch postponed to Q3 2026 (September) or possibly Q4 2026 due to ERP project immaturity. With RFP not launching until September 2026 at earliest, followed by shortlisting, vendor selection, negotiation, contracting, the February 2027 close is credible on calendar but buyer is not yet executing the planned next step (RFP launch). Last buyer touch was an email on 16 June 2026 stating no action before September 2026. Deal is live (recent contact) but stalled on a buyer-side dependency (ERP project decisions must complete before RFP scope can be defined). Time-in-stage: ~7 months in Formal Evaluation with no stage progression.
evidence: Next_Step__c history shows consistent slippage: RFP originally planned June 2026, slipped to Q3 2026 (September), now possibly Q4 2026 due to ERP project priority (source: Next_Step__c, 16 June 2026)

north_star_verdict.headline: Buyer-imposed stall continues through September 2026 due to ERP project dependency blocking RFP scope definition. Contact remains live with clear restart timeline, but zero competitive intelligence after 7 months. No execution until Q3/Q4 2026.

best_practice_check.summary: Three high-leverage levers to raise win probability: (1) Run competitive discovery when buyer re-engages in September 2026 so Zycus can tailor positioning and wedge strategy against the real shortlist. (2) Identify and cultivate a champion at Head of Procurement or CPO level with Economic Buyer access and vested interest in Zycus winning; current contacts are engaged but passive. (3) Multi-thread beyond the two OpportunityContactRole contacts to map the decision committee, including Economic Buyer, CFO/finance approver, IT/ERP lead, and Legal/InfoSec, before the RFP launches.

competitive_position.summary: No competitor intelligence has been captured. The buyer issued a formal RFI in November 2025, signaling a multi-vendor evaluation, but the competitive shortlist remains unknown. This is a significant blind spot 7 months into the cycle. Likely peer competitors in the European S2P market for a deal of this size include SAP Ariba, Coupa, Ivalua, GEP, Jaggaer, and Basware, but none are confirmed. Without competitive visibility, Zycus cannot tailor positioning, wedge strategy, or differentiation messaging. The single strongest current threat is UNKNOWN due to lack of discovery. The recommended action is to run competitive discovery as soon as the buyer re-engages in September 2026.

ai_positioning_strength.score: Strong feature demonstration, strategic alignment unknown
ai_positioning_strength.summary: Strong AI positioning demonstrated on 5 December 2025 product demo. Zycus showcased Merlin agentic AI layer with 45+ autonomous agents for supplier discovery, risk checks, onboarding, and tail-spend management; AI-guided intake routing via Microsoft Teams; and AI contract review flagging missing clauses and risk scores. Buyer engaged with AI use cases, asked questions about AI intake and compliance automation, and provided 'super positive feedback' per owner notes. However, buyer's own AI appetite, strategic AI priorities, and how AI fits their digital transformation roadmap are unknown because no executive or strategic buyer stakeholder has been engaged. The AI positioning is feature-strong but not yet anchored to a buyer-stated strategic imperative or quantified AI-driven ROI. Once the RFP launches and Economic Buyer is engaged, Zycus should co-develop an AI-led business case (autonomous tail-spend capture, zero-training Teams adoption, compliance automation ROI) to differentiate from competitors.

items.quote: Tail-spend management captures non-strategic 20% of spend to achieve 2–3% additional savings
items.source: Avoma product demo 5 December 2025, speaker_id 3 use case
items.criterion: Tail-spend management to capture non-strategic 20% of spend and achieve 2-3% additional savings

items.quote: Autonomous supplier onboarding automates discovery, risk assessment, data extraction, and ERP integration
items.source: Avoma product demo 5 December 2025, speaker_id 3 use case
items.criterion: Autonomous supplier onboarding with risk assessment, data extraction, and ERP integration

items.quote: Merlin Intake embedded in Microsoft Teams for zero-training user access; Business users query PO status, contract existence, or category spend via AI instead of manual requests
items.source: Avoma product demo 5 December 2025, speaker_id 3 use cases
items.criterion: AI-driven intake management embedded in Microsoft Teams for zero-training user adoption

items.quote: Bi-directional ERP synchronization for PO creation, goods receipt updates, and invoice/payment statuses
items.source: Avoma product demo 5 December 2025, integration plans
items.criterion: Bi-directional ERP synchronization for PO creation, goods receipt updates, and invoice/payment statuses

items.quote: Solution complies with GDPR and holds required security certifications
items.source: Avoma product demo 5 December 2025, compliance considerations
items.criterion: Compliance with GDPR and required security certifications

items.quote: Sanctions and embargo screening integrated in supplier risk radar for ongoing monitoring
items.source: Avoma product demo 5 December 2025, compliance considerations
items.criterion: Sanctions and embargo screening integrated for ongoing supplier risk monitoring

items.quote: AI contract review flags missing mandatory clauses (e.g., code of conduct, force majeure) and assigns risk scores
items.source: Avoma product demo 5 December 2025, compliance considerations
items.criterion: AI contract review to flag missing mandatory clauses and assign risk scores

items.quote: Teams-based invoice processing ingests email invoices via AP Smartdesk, detects duplicates, and automates matching
items.source: Avoma product demo 5 December 2025, use case
items.criterion: Teams-based invoice processing via email mailbox ingestion with duplicate detection and automated matching

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Strong product-feature fit demonstrated on 5 December 2025 demo. Buyer's stated priorities (tail-spend capture, autonomous supplier onboarding, Microsoft Teams-embedded AI intake, ERP bi-directional integration, compliance automation) align well with Zycus Merlin agentic AI, unified S2P suite on single database, and Teams integration. Buyer feedback on the demo was 'super positive' per owner notes. However, formal fit validation (BRD alignment, technical workshop, shortlist evaluation) has not occurred because RFP has not launched. Full expectations-fit assessment pending RFP scope definition in Q3/Q4 2026.

hard.ais_why:
• Avoma demo on 5 Dec 2025 with Mantu attendees Jeremy COURDIL, SELMANI Assia and Antoine Rodigue explicitly covered AI-driven intake routing via natural language, an Agentic AI layer with 45+ agents for autonomous supplier discovery / risk / onboarding / tail-spend, and Merlin Intake embedded in Teams for zero-training. Zycus committed in the call to send an RFI response including AI agent details ‚Äî the RFI explicitly requires AI capability detail, a strong evaluation-criterion signal.
• Formal Evaluation stage at $350K with broad AI-rich product scope (ANA, Agentic AI, Merlin, Merlin Intake) plus the full S2P suite (eInvoicing, eProcurement, iContract, iRequest, iRisk, iSave, iSupplier). 2-hour demo with Pierre Meraud, Brice Phosarajdavong and Hemendra Chaturvedi presenting. RFI underway with technical workshop scheduled. Customer Antoine engaged with detailed questions on agentic flow. Vienna March 10-12 customer event invitation extended.
• High confidence: direct AI-feature demo with named customer-side engagement, an RFI explicitly requiring AI capability detail, and AI-focused product scope. Held at 8 because BD_Engagement is Unengaged in SFDC (data lag ‚Äî engagement is clearly real) and no signed POC scope yet. A formal POC commitment or RFI shortlist confirmation would push to 9.

pulse.summary: Live: last verified Salesforce activity 10 day(s) ago (2026-06-16).

rep_outreach.note: rep reached out, awaiting buyer reply

deltas.subject: Run competitive discovery conversation as soon as buyer re-engages in September 2026. The buyer issued a formal RFI in November 2025, signaling multi-vendor evaluation, but the competitive shortlist is unknown 7 months into the cycle. Without visibility into who Zycus is competing against, positioning, differentiation messaging, and wedge strategy (Merlin agentic AI, unified single-database architecture, Microsoft Teams embedding) cannot be tailored. This is a critical blind spot. The recommended action is to explicitly ask Antoine Rodigue or Nicolas EUCHER who else is being evaluated and map their strengths, weaknesses, and buyer sentiment once the RFP timeline resumes. (source: Salesforce Competitors__c empty, Avoma 5 December 2025 no competitor discussed, Next_Step__c no competitor mentioned)

Identify and cultivate a true champion with Economic Buyer access and advocacy capability. Current buyer contacts (Nicolas EUCHER Procurement Manager, Jérémy COURDIL Data Quality Manager, Antoine Rodigue IT Category Manager) are engaged but passive; no evidence of internal selling, EB introductions, competitive intelligence sharing, or driving the RFP timeline has been captured. The risk is that once the RFP launches in Q3/Q4 2026, multiple vendors will compete on equal footing with no internal advocate tilting evaluation toward Zycus. The highest-leverage move once buyer re-engagement begins in September 2026 is to identify a champion at Head of Procurement or CPO level who has power, EB access, and a vested interest in Zycus winning, and co-develop the internal business case with them. (source: Avoma 5 December 2025 attendees functional-level only, Next_Step__c 16 June 2026 Antoine deferral message shows no advocacy)

deltas.subject: Multi-thread to map the decision committee before RFP launch. Only 2 buyer-side OpportunityContactRoles are on this opportunity (Nicolas EUCHER, Jérémy COURDIL), below the enterprise-deal multi-thread bar. The single recorded buyer call had the same two mid-level participants; no executive, no Economic Buyer, no IT/ERP leadership, no Legal/InfoSec, no CFO/finance approver has been engaged. For a €350k S2P transformation deal, the decision committee will be multi-functional and multi-level. The recommended action in July-August 2026 ahead of September re-engagement is to research Mantu's procurement and IT org structure on LinkedIn, identify the Head of Procurement, CFO, CIO/IT VP, and Legal/Procurement Ops leads, and request introductions from Antoine or Nicolas so Zycus can map decision criteria, approval gates, and Economic Buyer before the RFP is issued. Add these contacts as OpportunityContactRoles once confirmed involved. (source: Salesforce OpportunityContactRole count 2, Avoma 5 December 2025 attendees only 2 mid-level)
